why_current_is_wrong: Guaranteed Fit is classified as item_trust (Tier C) but it is a platform-wide transaction assurance  eBay backstops the promise with its own return/refund guarantee for misfit parts. No physical or digital item inspection occurs; the promise is made at the marketplace level.
why_fix_is_right: Slide 20 explicitly places Guaranteed Fit under Platform Trust alongside eBay MBG and Secure Purchase. The mechanism is buyer protection/resolution (eBay steps in if part does not fit), not item-level vetting or authentication.
rationale: Platform trust = marketplace-wide assurances eBay stands behind (slide 4). Guaranteed Fit is a platform-backed return guarantee, not an item authentication or accuracy vetting signal.
context_note: Distinct from condition verification. Fitment is about compatibility accuracy (platform promise), not item authentication.

why_current_is_wrong: Same signal as ebay-guaranteed-fit (US automotive parts fitment program). Classified as item_trust Tier C but no item-level vetting occurs; it is an eBay-backed return promise.
why_fix_is_right: Slide 20 places Guaranteed Fit under Platform Trust. This node duplicates the signal under a different parent (ebay-motors) but the classification logic is identical.
rationale: Platform-wide buyer protection for fitment; mechanism is resolution/refund, not item authentication. Slide 4, 20.
context_note: Duplicate context with ebay-guaranteed-fit under ebay-motors parent.

why_current_is_wrong: Classified as non_trust. Certified Refurbished is the top tier of eBay Refurbished  manufacturer-certified condition with a comprehensive warranty. It is a listing-level item trust signal.
why_fix_is_right: Sub-tier of eBay Refurbished which slide 20 places under Item Trust Tier C. Manufacturer certification of a specific item's condition is the definition of item trust accuracy (slide 9).
rationale: Item trust Tier C sub-tier. Manufacturer certification attaches to the specific item. Slide 4, 9, 13, 20.
context_note: Top condition tier of eBay Refurbished. Same trust layer as the parent program.

why_current_is_wrong: Classified as non_trust. eBay Refurbished is a four-tier certified refurbished program with professional inspections and warranties  it is a listing-level trust signal telling buyers the item has been vetted for condition and is backed by a guarantee.
why_fix_is_right: Slide 20 explicitly lists eBay Refurbished under Item Trust Tier C (Risk-Based Inventory Assessment). Slide 14 confirms Certified refurbished in Tier C. The program attaches to specific refurbished listings, making the promise item-level.
rationale: Item trust Tier C per slide 20. Listing-level condition vetting and warranty. Slide 4, 13, 14, 20.
context_note: This is the umbrella program. Its sub-tiers (Certified Refurbished, Excellent Refurbished, etc.) should also be item_trust.

why_current_is_wrong: UK equivalent of eBay Guaranteed Fit. Classified as item_trust Tier C; same misclassification  no item-level inspection occurs.
why_fix_is_right: Slide 20 shows Guaranteed Fit (and its equivalents) under Platform Trust. The UK variant follows the same platform-backed assurance model.
rationale: UK market variant of a platform-level fitment assurance. Slide 4, 20.
context_note: Market variant of Guaranteed Fit; classification logic identical.

why_current_is_wrong: UK fitment guarantee variant, branded as eBay Assured Fit. Classified as item_trust Tier C but no item inspection occurs  it is a platform refund/return promise.
why_fix_is_right: Slide 20 places Guaranteed Fit under Platform Trust. UK branding does not change the classification logic.
rationale: Platform assurance mechanism, not item authentication. Slide 4, 20.
context_note: Duplicate entry alongside assured-fit. Both describe the UK fitment promise.

why_current_is_wrong: Classified as item_trust Tier C. The 2-year Allstate warranty is a post-purchase assurance eBay provides across all eBay Refurbished listings  it is a platform-level buyer protection, not an item-level inspection or authentication signal.
why_fix_is_right: Slide 20 lists Free Warranty under Platform Trust alongside eMBG. A warranty backed by eBay and Allstate is a marketplace-wide assurance mechanism, matching the platform trust definition on slide 4.
rationale: Buyer protection/assurance delivered by eBay at the platform level. Not item-specific authentication. Slide 4, 20.
context_note: Distinct from the eBay Refurbished badge itself (item_trust Tier C). The warranty is the platform promise on top of the item signal.

why_current_is_wrong: Classified as platform_trust. The filter surfaces brand-authorized sellers  the underlying trust signal is seller-level (brand authorization is a seller-account attribute). However, the filter itself is a platform search feature.
why_fix_is_right: Slide 20 shows "Brand Auth. Seller" under Seller Trust. A filter that surfaces seller trust signals draws its classification from the underlying signal it exposes. Brand authorization is an account-level seller designation.
rationale: Surfaces seller-level brand authorization status. Primary object is the seller account. Slide 4, 20.
context_note: Ambiguous: the feature is a platform search tool, but the signal it exposes is seller_trust. Recommend move to seller_trust since the classification should follow the trust object, not the UI element.

why_current_is_wrong: Classified as platform_trust. Late Shipment Rate is a seller account performance metric tracking how often a specific seller ships late. It does not apply marketplace-wide  it varies by seller.

context_note: Feeds TRS and Top Rated Plus eligibility. Same trust layer as those signals.

why_current_is_wrong: Classified as platform_trust. Valid Tracking Rate is a seller performance metric (percentage of shipments with valid tracking by that seller). It varies by seller account, not platform-wide.
why_fix_is_right: Slide 4: seller trust = account-level indicators of reliability. Tracking rate measures seller fulfillment behavior per account. Required for Top Rated status (seller trust signal per slide 20).
rationale: Seller-account fulfillment metric. Slide 4.
context_note: Required for TRS eligibility. Seller trust layer.

context_note: Ambiguous: eBay provides the report as a platform service, but the content is item-specific. The object of the trust promise is the vehicle item  classify as item_trust accuracy.

why_current_is_wrong: Classified as platform_trust. CCG (PCGS, NGC, CGC) provides item-level grading and authentication for coins, comics, and collectibles. Their certifications attach to specific items, not to the platform or seller account.
why_fix_is_right: Slide 13 Tier A: physically authenticated by a trusted 3rd party partner. CCG members are exactly this  grading and authentication applied to individual items. Their certification is the item trust signal buyers see.

context_note: PCGS, NGC, CGC are sub-brands of CCG. Each grades individual items. Same logic as PSA for trading cards.

why_current_is_wrong: Classified as platform_trust. Sneaker Con authenticates individual sneakers as part of eBay AG  the authentication outcome attaches to the specific pair of sneakers, not to the platform or the seller.
why_fix_is_right: Slide 13 Tier A: physically authenticated by eBay authenticator or trusted 3rd party. Sneaker Con is a 3rd party authentication partner whose verdict applies to each specific item. Slide 15 shows sneaker authentication as Tier A.
rationale: Item-level 3rd party authentication partner for sneakers. Slide 4, 13, 15.
context_note: Authentication partner in the AG ecosystem. Result attaches to the individual sneaker listing.
